resources.name: Action metrics and errors
resources.description: Metrics and errors related to the actions, including both import/export processes and the sending or receiving of events within the pipelines.

endpoints.name: Get metrics per dates
endpoints.description: Retrieves metrics for actions aggregated by day for a time interval between specified start and end dates.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
The starting date in ISO 8601 format. It must be earlier than the end date.

Additionally, the date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The ending date in ISO 8601 format. It must be later than the start date.

Additionally, the date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which metrics should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in this way:

`actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The ending date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that successfully passed each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that failed at each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: days

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:

The actions can be specified in the query string in this way:

parameters.name: start

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The ending date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that successfully passed each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that failed at each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

endpoints.name: Get errors
endpoints.description: Retrieves errors for actions for a time interval between specified start and end dates.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
The starting date in ISO 8601 format. It must be earlier than the end date.

Additionally, the date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The ending date in ISO 8601 format. It must be later than the start date.

Additionally, the date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which errors should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in this way:

`actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: The pipeline step for which errors should be returned. If no step is specified, errors will be returned for all steps.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of errors to skip before starting to return errors. It must be between 0 and 9999, with a default value of 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of errors to return. It must be between 1 and 100, with a default value of 100.

parameters.name: errors

properties.name: action
properties.description: The action in which the error occurred.

properties.name: step
properties.description: The step where the error occurred.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of occurrences of the error within the specified period.

properties.name: message
properties.description: The error message.

properties.name: lastOccurred
properties.description: The date of the last occurrence of the error within the specified period.

parameters.description: The errors that occurred during the specified period for the provided actions.

resources.name: Export users to apps
resources.description: This type of action exports user data from the workspace's data warehouse to an application. It operates on a destination app connection that supports users.

endpoints.name: Create action
endpoints.description: Create a destination action that exports users to an app.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The ID of the connection to which the users will be written. It must be a destination app connection that exports users.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The entity on which the action operates, which must be `"User"` in order to create an action that exports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a `json` type, it can include a json path.

properties.name: operator

values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the app users. If it's not null, only the users that match the filter will be included.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: matching

properties.name: in
properties.description:
The matching input property. It cannot be empty.

It represents the name of the property in the user schema. Its definition must also be included in the action's input schema.

properties.name: out
properties.description:
The matching output property. It cannot be empty.

It represents the name of the property in the app's user schema. Its definition must also be included in the action's output schema.

parameters.description: The properties used to identify the match between a user in the workspace and a user in the app. These properties are required to determine which users should be updated and which should be created as new in the app.

parameters.name: exportMode
parameters.description:
The mode in which users are exported:

* `"CreateOnly"`: Only new users are created in the app. No existing users are modified.
* `"UpdateOnly"`: Only existing users are updated in the app. No new users are created.
* `"CreateOrUpdate"`: If a user already exists in the app, they are updated; otherwise, they are created as a new user.

It should not be set to `"CreateOnly"` and `"CreateOrUpdate"` if the matching output property is read-only; if it is, user creation will fail because the matching output property must be writable.

parameters.name: updateOnDuplicates
parameters.description:
Determines whether, when multiple app users match a single user in the workspace's data warehouse, they should still be updated.

If set to true, the update will proceed regardless of duplicates, otherwise the duplicated users will not be updated, and an error will be logged. The default value is false.

This field does not affect user creation.

parameters.name: transformation

properties.name: mapping
properties.description: The transformation mapping. A key represents a path of a property in the destination schema of the app, and its corresponding value is an expression. This expression can reference property paths from the user schema.

properties.name: function
source
properties.description: The source code of the JavaScript or Python function.

properties.name: language
properties.description: The language of the function.

properties.name: preserveJSON
properties.description: Specifies whether JSON values are passed to and returned from the function as strings, keeping their original format without any encoding or decoding.

properties.name: inPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that will be passed to the function. At least one path must be present.

properties.name: outPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that may be returned by the function. At least one path must be present.
The transformation function. A JavaScript or Python function that given a workspace's user, returns an app user.

parameters.description:
The mapping or function responsible for transforming unified users into app users.

One of either a mapping or a function must be provided, but not both. The one that is not provided can be either missing or set to null.

parameters.name: inSchema
parameters.description:
The schema of the input matching property, the properties used in the filter, and the input properties within the transformation.

When exporting users to apps, it should be a subset of user schema.

parameters.name: outSchema
parameters.description:
The schema of the output matching property and the output properties within the transformation.

When exporting users to apps, it should be a subset of app's destination schema, also including the schema of the output matching property if it is not already present.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

resources.name: Export users to databases
resources.description: This type of action exports user data from the workspace's data warehouse to a database table. It operates on a destination database connection.

endpoints.name: Create action
endpoints.description: Create a destination action that exports users to a database.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The ID of the connection to which the users will be written. It must be a destination database.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The entity on which the action operates, which must be `"User"` in order to create an action that exports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: tableName
parameters.description: The name of the table where the users will be exported.

parameters.name: tableKey
parameters.description:
The name of the table column that contains a value identifying a user within the table. This column must be included in the output schema.

Typically, this is the column used as the table's primary key. However, it can also be a column with a unique constraint, or one that is guaranteed to contain only unique values.

If a row with the same value in this column already exists, it will be updated; otherwise, a new row will be created for the exported user.

The type of this column must match one of the following Meergo types: `int`, `uint`, `uuid`, or `text`.

parameters.name: transformation

properties.name: mapping
properties.description: The transformation mapping. A key represents the name of a column in the database table, and its corresponding value is an expression. This expression can reference property paths from the user schema.

properties.name: function
source
properties.description: The source code of the JavaScript or Python function.

properties.name: language
properties.description: The language of the function.

properties.name: preserveJSON
properties.description: Specifies whether JSON values are passed to and returned from the function as strings, keeping their original format without any encoding or decoding.

properties.name: inPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that will be passed to the function. At least one path must be present.

properties.name: outPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that may be returned by the function. At least one path must be present.
A JavaScript or Python function that, given a workspace user, returns a user record to be written to the database table.

parameters.description:
The mapping or function responsible for transforming unified users into database users.

One of either a mapping or a function must be provided, but not both. The one that is not provided can be either missing or set to null.

parameters.name: inSchema
parameters.description:
The schema of the properties used in the filter and the input properties within the transformation.

It should be a subset of the user schema.

parameters.name: outSchema
parameters.description:
The schema for the table key property and the output properties within the transformation.

In addition to the table key property, it must contain at least one other output property.

When exporting users to a database, this should be a subset of the database table schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

resources.name: Export users to files
resources.description: This type of action exports user data from the workspace's data warehouse to a newly created file. It operates on a destination file storage connection.

endpoints.name: Create action
endpoints.description: Create a destination action that exports users to a file.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The ID of the connection to which the file will be written. It must be a destination file storage connection.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The entity on which the action operates, which must be `"User"` in order to create an action that exports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. It corresponds to the name of a file connector.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path relative to the root path defined in the file storage. Refer to the file storage documentation for details on the specific format.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description:
The sheet name. It can only be used with the Excel format, where it is required.

When provided, it must have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description: The format used to compress the file. If not provided or empty, the file will remain uncompressed.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: The property path by which users will be ordered in the exported file.

parameters.name: formatSettings
parameters.description:
The settings for the file format. Refer to the documentation for the [connector](/connectors/) related to the file format to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the file format does not require any settings, the `formatSettings` field must be either omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a `json` type, it can include a json path.

properties.name: operator

values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the users in the file. If it's not null, only the users that match the filter will be included.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: inSchema
parameters.description: The schema of the properties in the filter, along with the other properties from the user schema, to be exported into the file.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

endpoints.name: Create action
endpoints.description: Create a source action to import users from an app.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The ID of the connection from which to read the users. It must be a source app that can import users.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The entity on which the action operates, which must be `"User"` in order to create an action that imports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a `json` type, it can include a json path.

values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the app users. If it's not null, only the app users that match the filter will be included, otherwise all users will be included.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: incremental
parameters.description:
Determines whether users are imported incrementally:
* `true`: imports only users who were created or updated since the last import.
* `false`: imports all users, regardless of previous imports.

parameters.name: transformation

properties.name: mapping
properties.description: The transformation mapping. A key represents a property path in the user schema, and its corresponding value is an expression. This expression can reference property paths from the source schema of the app.

properties.name: function
source
properties.description: The source code of the JavaScript or Python function.

properties.name: language
properties.description: The language of the function.

properties.name: preserveJSON
properties.description: Specifies whether JSON values are passed to and returned from the function as strings, keeping their original format without any encoding or decoding.

properties.name: inPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that will be passed to the function. At least one path must be present.

properties.name: outPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that may be returned by the function. At least one path must be present.
The transformation function. A JavaScript or Python function that given an app user, returns a user identity.

parameters.description:
The mapping or function responsible for transforming app users into user identities linked to the action. Once the identity resolution process is complete, the user identities associated with all actions are merged into unified users.

One of either a mapping or a function must be provided, but not both. The one that is not provided can be either missing or set to null.

parameters.name: inSchema
parameters.description:
The schema for the properties used in the filter, as well as the input properties for the transformation.

When importing users from apps, this should be a subset of the app's destination schema.

parameters.name: outSchema
parameters.description:
The schema for the output properties of the transformation.

When importing users from apps, this should be a subset of the user schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

resources.name: Import users from databases
resources.description: This type of action imports user data from a database into the workspace's data warehouse. It operates on a source database connection.

endpoints.name: Create action
endpoints.description: Create a source action to import users from a database.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The ID of the connection from which to read the users. It must be a source database.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The entity on which the action operates, which must be `"User"` in order to create an action that imports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: query
parameters.placeholder: "SELECT name, email, updated_at FROM customers WHERE ${last_change_time}"
parameters.description:
The SELECT query executed on the database to retrieve the users to import.

The column names returned must be valid property names. They must:
- Start with `A`...`Z`, `a`...`z`, or `_`,
- Contain only `A`...`Z`, `a`...`z`, `0`...`9`, or `_`.

Note that you can use a column alias if necessary (e.g., `SELECT 1 AS v FROM users`).

The query should include the `last_change_time` placeholder as a condition in the WHERE clause.

parameters.name: identityColumn
parameters.description:
The column that uniquely identifies each user in the database. It serves as the single, unique identifier for each user record, ensuring that each user can be distinctly referenced.

Only columns with types corresponding to the following Meergo types can be used as an identity: `int`, `uint`, `uuid`, `json`, and `text`.

parameters.name: lastChangeTimeColumn
parameters.description:
The column that stores the date when a user record was last updated. It tracks the most recent modification made to the user's data, helping to identify when changes occurred.

The value of this column is used for incremental imports, where only records that have been modified since the last import need to be processed.

Only columns with types corresponding to the following Meergo types can be used as the last change time: `datetime`, `date`, `json`, and `text`.

parameters.name: lastChangeTimeFormat
parameters.description:
The format of the value in the last change time column. It can be set to `"ISO8601"` if the column value follows the ISO 8601 format. Otherwise, it should follow a format accepted by the [Python strftime function](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#strftime-strptime-behavior).

This field is only required if the `lastChangeTimeColumn` is provided, is not empty, and has a type `json` or `text`.

parameters.name: incremental
parameters.description:
Determines whether users are imported incrementally:
* `true`: are imported only users whose last change time is equal to or later than the last imported user's change time.
* `false`: all users are imported again, regardless of their last change time. `false` is the default value.

If set to `true`, a column for the last change time must be specified (i.e., `lastChangeTimeColumn` is not null). Additionally, if the last change time column type corresponds to the Meergo `json` or `text` types, the values in the column must be sortable.

parameters.name: transformation

properties.name: mapping
properties.description: The transformation mapping. A key represents a property path in the user schema, and its corresponding value is an expression. This expression can reference columns of the database table.

properties.name: function
source
properties.description: The source code of the JavaScript or Python function.

properties.name: language
properties.description: The language of the function.

properties.name: preserveJSON
properties.description: Specifies whether JSON values are passed to and returned from the function as strings, keeping their original format without any encoding or decoding.

properties.name: inPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that will be passed to the function. At least one path must be present.

properties.name: outPaths
properties.description: The paths of the properties that may be returned by the function. At least one path must be present.
The transformation function. A JavaScript or Python function that given a user in the database, returns a user identity.

parameters.description:
The mapping or function responsible for transforming database users into user identities linked to the action. Once the identity resolution process is complete, the user identities associated with all actions are merged into unified users.

One of either a mapping or a function must be provided, but not both. The one that is not provided can be either missing or set to null.

parameters.name: inSchema
parameters.description:
The schema for the identity column, the last change time column, and the input properties for the transformation.

When importing users from databases, this should be a subset of the query schema.

parameters.name: outSchema
parameters.description:
The schema for the output properties of the transformation.

When importing users from databases, this should be a subset of the user schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

resources.name: Import users from files
resources.description: This type of action imports user data from a file into the workspace's data warehouse. It operates on a source file storage connection.

endpoints.name: Create action
endpoints.description: Create a source action that imports users from a file.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The ID of the connection from which to read the users. It must be a source file storage.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The entity on which the action operates, which must be `"User"` in order to create an action that imports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. It corresponds to the name of a file connector.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path relative to the root path defined in the file storage connection. Refer to the file storage connector documentation for details on the specific format.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description:
The sheet name. It can only be used with the Excel format, where it is required.

When provided, it must have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description:
The compression format of the file. It is empty if the file is not compressed.

Note that an Excel file is inherently compressed, so no compression format needs to be specified unless the file has been further compressed.

parameters.name: formatSettings
parameters.description:
The settings for the file format. Refer to the documentation for the [file connector](/connectors/) related to the file format to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the file format does not require any settings, the `formatSettings` field must be either omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property

is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the users in the file. If it's not null, only the users that match the filter will be included.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: identityColumn
parameters.description:
The column that uniquely identifies each user in the file. It serves as the single, unique identifier for each user record, ensuring that each user can be distinctly referenced.

Only columns with types corresponding to the following Meergo types can be used as an identity: `int`, `uint`, `uuid`, `json`, and `text`.

parameters.name: lastChangeTimeColumn
parameters.description:
The column that stores the date when a user record was last updated. It tracks the most recent modification made to the user's data, helping to identify when changes occurred.

The value of this column is used for incremental imports, where only records that have been modified since the last import need to be processed.

Only columns with types corresponding to the following Meergo types can be used as the last change time: `datetime`, `date`, `json`, and `text`.

parameters.name: lastChangeTimeFormat
parameters.description:
The format of the value in the last change time column. It can be set to `"ISO8601"` if the column value follows the ISO 8601 format. If `format` is `"Excel"`, it can also be set to `"Excel"`. Otherwise, it should follow a format accepted by the [Python strftime function](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#strftime-strptime-behavior).

This field is only required if the `lastChangeTimeColumn` is provided, is not empty, and has a type `json` or `text`.

parameters.name: incremental
parameters.description:
Determines whether users are imported incrementally:
* `true`: are imported only users whose last change time is equal to or later than the last imported user's change time.
* `false`: all users are imported again, regardless of their last change time. `false` is the default value.

If set to `true`, a column for the last change time must be specified (i.e., `lastChangeTimeColumn` is not null).

resources.name: Actions
resources.description:
Actions represent the operations that can be performed on [connections](connections), such as importing and exporting users or storing and sending events.

This section documents the endpoints common to various types of actions. For creating, updating, and retrieving an action, refer to the specific sections for each type of action.

endpoints.name: Set status
endpoints.description: Sets the status of an action.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action that imports or exports users.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicates if the action is enabled.

endpoints.name: Set schedule period
endpoints.description: Sets the frequency at which an action imports or exports users. The action must be enabled for the execution to run as scheduled. If the period is null, the scheduler will be disabled.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action that imports or exports users.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: The schedule period. It determines how often the execution runs automatically. If it is null, the scheduler will be disabled, and no automatic executions will occur.

endpoints.name: Execute action
endpoints.description:
Starts an action execution to import its users into the data warehouse or export the users in the data warehouse to the app, applying the action's filter and transformation.

It returns immediately without waiting for the execution to complete. To track the progress, call the [Get execution](#get-execution) endpoint using the returned execution ID.

The action must be enabled.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action that imports or exports users.

parameters.name: incremental
parameters.description:
Determines whether users should be imported from scratch (`false`) or incrementally from the previous import (`true`).  If omitted or nil, the action's default setting will be used.

For source database and file actions, the action must include a "last change time" property.

For destination actions, this parameter must be omitted or set to nil.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the started execution.

errors.message: incremental requires a last change time column
data warehouse is in inspection mode
data warehouse is in maintenance mode

properties.name: failed

properties.description: An error occurred during execution, causing it to stop prematurely. It is empty if the execution has not yet finished or if no error occurred.

resources.name: Connections
resources.description:
Connections serve as a channel between workspaces and external sources or destinations, such as applications, databases, file storage, websites, mobile apps, and servers.

[Actions](actions) then allow you to perform operations on these connections.

endpoints.name: Create connection
endpoints.description:
Creates a connection.

For connectors that require authorization, follow these steps to create the connection:

1. [Get the auth URL](connections#get-auth-url) and redirect the user to it.
2. Once the user grants permission, [retrieve the auth token](connections#retrieve-auth-token).
3. Create the connection by passing the auth token as the `authToken` argument.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The connection's name.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Indicates if the connection is a data source or a data destination.

parameters.name: connector
parameters.description:
The name of the [connector](/connectors/) for which to create the connection. It can be an app, database, file storage or SDK connector, but cannot be a file connector or a stream connector.

Stream connections will be available soon.

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: The [strategy](/identity-resolution/anonymous-users-strategies) for anonymous users. It is required and can only be provided for source SDK connections that use them.

parameters.name: sendingMode
parameters.description:
The mode for sending events. It can be one of the sending modes supported by the app.

It is required and can only be provided with destination app connections that support it.

parameters.name: linkedConnections
parameters.description:
The IDs of the connections to which events are sent or from which events are received.

For source connections (SDK), the linked connections are the destination app connections where the received events are forwarded. On the other hand, for destination app connections, the linked connections are the source SDK connections from which events are received.

If this field is null, it means there are no linked connections, or the connection type does not match any of the types mentioned above.

parameters.name: settings
parameters.description:
The specific settings of the connection, which vary based on the connector specified in the `connector` field. Please refer to the documentation for the [connector](/connectors/) to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the connector does not require any settings, the `settings` field may be omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: authToken
parameters.description: The authorization token. Is required if the connector requires authorization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the connection.

endpoints.description: Returns the user schema of the workspace.

endpoints.description: Alters the user schema of the workspace.

parameters.description: The new user schema. It must include at least one property.

parameters.name: primarySources
parameters.description:
The primary source for each schema property that has one, where the key is the property name and the value is the connection identifier.

This source defines where the definitive value for the property is read from, preventing other sources from overwriting it once it is set.

If no primary source is provided, the new schema will have no primary sources defined.

parameters.name: rePaths
parameters.description:
Specifies renamed properties and additional information that cannot be expressed through the `schema` parameter alone.

In particular:

- If a property in `schema` has been renamed, the new path must be added as a key in `rePaths` and the old path as the associated value. Otherwise, instead of performing a rename operation, a new property with the new path would be created, and the property with the old path would be deleted.
- If a property in `schema` has been added with the same path of an already existent one which should be removed, then the path of the new property must be added as a key in `rePaths` and `null` as the associated value. Otherwise, instead of creating a new property and deleting the old one, it would be interpreted as a rename operation.

endpoints.name: Get information about the latest user schema alteration
endpoints.description:
Returns information about the latest altering of the user schema.

Depending on the returned values:
- If neither `startTime` nor `endTime` are returned, it means that no alteration of the user schema has ever been performed for the workspace.
- If only `startTime` is returned, it means that the workspace is currently running a user schema alteration.
- If both `startTime` and `endTime` are returned, it means that a user schema alteration has been performed and no user schema alteration is currently running.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description:
Start timestamp (UTC) of the latest user schema update, either running or completed.

If null, no user schema update has ever been started for the workspace.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description:
End timestamp (UTC) for the latest user schema update.

If null, it means that the user schema update is still in progress, or that no schema update has ever been performed for the workspace.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description:
A possible error in the execution of the latest update of the user schema.

If null, it means that no update of the user schema has ever been executed, or that one is in progress, or that the last one executed completed without errors.

resources.name: Event write keys
resources.description:
Event write keys are used for authentication when sending events from websites, mobile apps, and servers through the [Ingest event](events#ingest-event) and [Ingest events](events#ingest-events) endpoints.

Keys for SDK connections that receive events from a website or a mobile are usually public, as they can be exposed in a website's source code or on a mobile device. In contrast, keys for SDK connections used on servers should always remain private.

resources.name: Events
resources.description:
Events are customer behavioral events (such as page views, clicks, or purchases) received from websites, mobile apps, and servers. They can be stored in the workspace's data warehouse and forwarded to apps. You can also import customer data for identity resolution and unification.

These endpoints allow you to ingest events, retrieve events from the data warehouse, get the event schema, and manage event listeners.

You can also use one of the available [SDKs to send events](/developers/), instead of interacting with these API endpoints directly.

endpoints.name: Ingest events
endpoints.description:
This endpoint allows you to ingest a batch of events.
### Authentication
It supports authentication with both an [API key](authentication) and an [event write key](authentication#event-write-keys):
* For a website or mobile app, you must exclusively use an event write key, as it only provides access to event ingestion endpoints.
* For a server application, using an event write key is recommended if you don't need access to other endpoints.
### Alternative payloads
Alternatively, when using an event write key, you can also pass the array of events directly in the request body.

If there is a single event to ingest, you can pass the event object directly, similar to the [Ingest event](events#ingest-event) endpoint, but specifying the event type in the request body.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description:
The ID of the connection to which the events refer. It can only be a source SDK connection.

It is required only if the call is authenticated using an API key. If authentication is done with an event write key, it is not needed, as the connection is that of the key.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
The file path relative to the root of the file storage. For details on the required format, refer to the file storage connector documentation.

The path must not be empty and cannot exceed 1,024 characters in length.

Note that the path must be URL-encoded. For example, if the path is `docs/users/subscribers.csv`, you need to pass it as `docs%2Fusers%2Fsubscribers.csv`.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. Note that it corresponds to the name of the file connector used to read the file.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description:
The sheet name. It can only be used with the Excel format, where it is required.

When provided, it must have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description:
The method used to compress the file, if applicable.

**Note:** An Excel file is inherently compressed, so no compression method needs to be specified unless the file has been further compressed.

parameters.name: formatSettings
parameters.description:

resources.name: Identity resolution
resources.description: Identities are the users obtained from sources like [apps](app-connections), [databases](database-connections), [files](file-storage-connections), or events. These identities are filtered and transformed according to a defined action. Once identity resolution is complete, the collected identities from all [connections](connections) are unified to form the workspace [users](users).

endpoints.name: Start identity resolution
endpoints.description: Starts identity resolution that resolves the identities of the workspace.

errors.message: data warehouse is in inspection mode
data warehouse is in maintenance mode

endpoints.name: Get information about latest identity resolution
endpoints.description:
Returns information about the latest identity resolution.

Depending on the returned values:
- If neither `startTime` nor `endTime` are returned, it means that no identity resolutions have ever been performed for the workspace.
- If only `startTime` is returned, it means that the workspace is currently running an identity resolution.
- If both `startTime` and `endTime` are returned, it means that an identity resolution has been performed and there are no identity resolutions currently running.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description:
Start timestamp (UTC) of the latest identity resolution, either running or completed.

If null, no identity resolutions have ever been executed for the workspace.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description:
End timestamp (UTC) for the latest identity resolution.

If null, it means that the identity resolution is still in progress, or that no identity resolution has ever been executed on the workspace.

parameters.name: runOnBatchImport
parameters.description: Indicates if identity resolution is automatically run when a batch import is completed. The default is false.

parameters.name: identifiers
parameters.description:
The identifiers of the identity resolution, ordered from the highest precedence to the lowest precedence.

An identifier must be a property path that refers to a property of the user schema of the workspace. The referred property must have type `int`, `uint`, `uuid`, `inet`, `text`, or `decimal` with zero scale. Identifiers cannot be repeated.

Not specifying any identifier means performing identity resolution without comparing any identifiers.

errors.message: alter schema is in execution so the identifiers cannot be updated
identity resolution is in execution so the identifiers cannot be updated
property does not exist in the user schema
a property has a type which is not allowed for identifiers

parameters.name: runOnBatchImport

parameters.name: identifiers
parameters.description:
The identifiers of the identity resolution, ordered from the highest precedence to the lowest precedence.

If no identifiers are returned, it means that the identity resolution is performed without comparing any identifiers.

resources.name: Users
resources.description: Users are those imported from external sources and events, unified through identity resolution, and stored in the workspace's data warehouse.

endpoints.name: Retrieve all users
endpoints.description: Retrieves users stored in the workspace's data warehouse, up to a maximum number of users defined by `limit`. You must specify which properties to include. If a filter is provided, only users that match the filter criteria will be returned.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description:
The names of the properties to return. At least one property must be included.

The properties can be specified in query string in this way:
```
properties=first_name&properties=last_name&properties=email
```

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a `json` type, it can include a json path.

properties.name: operator

values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the users. Only the users that match the filter will be returned.

It must be encoded in JSON, then escaped for the context of the query string. So, for example, the JSON-encoded filter:

`{"logical":"and","conditions":[{"property":"email","operator":"is","values":["my.friend@example.com"]}]}`

must then be escaped and passed in the query string as:

`filter=%7B%22logical%22%3A%22and%22%2C%22conditions
%22%3A%5B%7B%22property%22%3A%22email%22%2C%22
operator%22%3A%22is%22%2C%22values%22%3A%5B%22
my.friend%40example.com%22%5D%7D%5D%7D`

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The name of the property by which to sort the users to be returned. It can be any property from the user schema with a sortable type, meaning it cannot be of type `json`, `array`, `object`, or `map`.

parameters.name: orderDesc
parameters.description: Indicates if the returned users are sorted in descending order; if not `true`, they are sorted in ascending order.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of users to skip before starting to return results. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of users to return. It can be any value between 1 and 1,000. If not provided, the default value is 100.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The ID of the user.

properties.name: sourcesLastUpdate
properties.description:
The date and time when the user's data was last updated on the sources. It corresponds to the most recent last update of its identities.

Its value is independent of which properties were requested.

properties.name: traits
properties.description: The traits of the user. Only the properties explicitly requested are included.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The schema of the returned traits. It corresponds to the user schema but includes only the properties that were explicitly requested.

parameters.name: total
parameters.description: An estimate of the total number of users, without considering `first` and `limit`.

endpoints.name: Retrieve user events
endpoints.description:
Retrieves the most recent events for a user given their identifier. The events are read from the workspace's data warehouse and are listed in descending order, starting with the most recent ones.

This endpoint provides a streamlined, user-focused alternative to the [Retrieve all events](events#retrieve-all-events) endpoint.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description:
The names of the event properties to return. At least one property must be included.

The properties can be specified in the query string in two ways:
* `properties=timestamp,event`
* `properties=timestamp&properties=event`

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of events to return. It can be any value between 1 and 1,000. If not provided, the default value is 100.

parameters.name: events

properties.name: channel
properties.description: The channel from which the event originates.

properties.name: context
location
properties.description: Device location from which the event originated.

properties.name: network
properties.description: Network to which the device originating the event was connected.

properties.name: os
properties.description: OS of the device from which the event originated.
Additional contextual information for the event. If there's no information in the context, this field is not returned.

properties.name: event
properties.description: Field that identifies a track-type event, for example with a value like `Product Purchased`. For any other event type, it is never returned.

properties.name: messageId
properties.description:
ID that uniquely identifies the event for a given connection. Automatically generated by Meergo when the event is received.

Unlike `id`, which uniquely identifies the event across all connections, this identifies the event uniquely only for a specific connection.

properties.name: name
properties.description: It is returned only for screen and page events.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: A JSON Object representing the properties associated with the event. Returned only for page, screen, and track events.

properties.name: receivedAt
properties.description: Event reception timestamp. Automatically set by Meergo when the event is received.

properties.name: sentAt
properties.description: Event send timestamp. If not provided with the event, one is automatically generated by Meergo upon event receipt.

properties.name: userId
properties.description: ID of the user who generated the event, if available; otherwise, this field is not returned. Not to be confused with user, which refers to the internal Meergo user ID associated with the event by the Identity Resolution.

parameters.description: The most recent events of the user. An empty array is returned if no events are available or if the specified user does not exist.

endpoints.name: Retrieve user identities
endpoints.description:
Retrieves, from the workspace's data warehouse, the identities of a user given its identifier.

Identities are sorted by last change time, in descending order, so the most recently changed identities are returned first.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of identities to skip before starting to return results. The default value is 0.

parameters.description: The maximum number of identities to return. It can be any value between 1 and 1,000. If not provided, the default value is 100.

parameters.name: identities

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The ID of the connection through which the identity was read.

properties.name: action
properties.description: The ID of the action through which the identity was imported.

properties.name: id
properties.description:
The unique identifier that represents the identity in the source from which it was retrieved. This field is empty for anonymous users. Specifically:
* For an SDK connection, it corresponds to the User ID of the event. This field is empty for identities imported from anonymous events.
* For an app connection, it is the value used to identify the user in the app. For example, in HubSpot, it corresponds to the HubSpot ID.
* For a database or file storage connection, it is the value in the column designated as the identity.

properties.name: anonymousIds
properties.description: The anonymous IDs of the identity. It is null for identities not imported from events.

properties.name: lastChangeTime
properties.description: The identity's most recent change time.

parameters.description: The user's identities, containing at least one identity.

properties.name: resolveIdentitiesOnBatchImport
properties.description:
Indicates whether identity resolution should be performed immediately after a batch import.

If set to true, Meergo will automatically resolve identities as soon as the import is finished.

properties.description:
The user schema properties used to determine if two users are the same. The identity resolution checks these identities in order.
Two users are considered the same if they have the same value for the first identity, or if neither user has a value for the first identity, the identity resolution moves to the next one, and so on.

If no identifiers are specified, once identity resolution is performed, all users will be considered different from each other.
